title: phyllanthus rupicola
intro: Phyllanthus rupicola, also known as the rocky tree-phyllanthus, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Phyllanthaceae, characterized by its woody stems and small, white flowers. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Phyllanthus rupicola include its ability to support liver health, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including liver and kidney problems.
